Alright, now I feel like I'm at home And when I'm going, don't worry about offending me When my audience does it, they are borderline disrespectful Right, I'm just hearing it all the time, but that lets me know I'm in the right place doing the right thing Hey, the last thing I want to ask you, I want to talk about is as we're teaching and as we're talking, because I'm doing both today I want to be vulnerable with you Can I be vulnerable? Yes! And when I talk about being vulnerable, one of the first things I want to talk about is I mentioned scaling through live events Is the first live event we ever did with traffic sales and profit It was June 23, 2016 And when we set the event up, we said we're going to have 200 people, how many? 200 200, I want to see that in the chat, 200 people Then as we got closer, we said it don't look like 200 people about to come to this So we went from 200 people to 100 people, anybody ever had the downsides of the dream? So we went from 200 to 100, but then guess what, do you think we had 100 people? We did not So that morning of the event, I remember me and Ronnie in the room pulling out chairs Because we didn't want the people to think they were crazy and they were fools for being the only ones that bought tickets Anybody ever put chairs out of a room before because you didn't have enough people? Nobody, okay, okay, my people So not only did we not hit 100, we ended up having 47 people And the wildest part about having 47 people is that half of them had free tickets We had actually sold a course the year before and we gave them a free ticket to the conference So we went from 200 to 100, 100 to 47 with only 27 of those people actually having paid us Now at this event, we had a plan and the plan was that we would sell our first ever high ticket offer It was going to be a $10,000 year long mastermind and we were afraid Anybody ever been fearful, let me know in the chat, right? You got your first 1000, your 5000, your $10,000, your $100,000 offer And you are just scared out of your mind that nobody's going to buy it And I had this thought, like, what if I get on the stage and I tell them, hey, go to the back of the room and nobody goes I told, you know, one of my team members, like, look, just get up and walk back there Like, if nobody goes, like, you go, like, I cannot go out like this And what happened to make it even worse is during the event, right? That time it was a three day event we did Thursday, Friday, Saturday During the event on Thursday, we found out that my salesperson, my best salesperson, my only salesperson Y'all know who this was? My wife, right? Had to have surgery all of a sudden And they said, you have to have surgery tomorrow on Saturday And Saturday was going to be the day that we were actually selling Now, all of a sudden, I'm imagining, I'm selling from the stage, I'm sending people to the back of the room Ronnie's supposed to be closing them in the back of the room, but guess what? Now we know that Ronnie isn't even going to be there And not only this, right? While I'm delivering all this information, I'm worried about my wife I'm worried about what she's going through, and we can't cancel the event The event has to go on, people are already there, the contracts are already signed And our financial livelihood is tied into the success of this event Anybody ever been there before? And I realize all of a sudden, our personal finances are tied into the business Now what they say is, they say all the time, you shouldn't mix your personal money with your business money But for many of us, the personal money is the only money we have to actually start the business Anybody ever been there before? So we did it, we made it happen, three days through the grace of God, guess what? We hit the goal Ten people joined, ten thousand dollars each, and we made it There we go, Michael, Janine, I appreciate the nuggets I don't know what's going on in the room, I haven't got a nugget yet, just keep working through the process Alright, so we went from that 47 people in the room to now we've grown to a thousand people, right? Twice a year, every January and June in a town called Alpharetta outside of Atlanta Now the thing is, not only have we grown to a thousand people, but we've been blessed to meet and work with And bring in some of the entrepreneurs and individuals that we've only dreamed about visiting, meeting, working with And building relationships over the years And what's even better than that, y'all, you want to know what's even better than having a thousand attendees in your events Is having hundreds of those people signed up and become not just attendees, but actual customers And I'm not just becoming customers, but how would you like to have people excited today that spent 25, 30, 50 thousand dollars with you? Hundreds of people is not only excited, but they're excited not about the investment, because the investment may make them nervous The investment they could be afraid of, but they're excited because they know that they're on the first step of a transformation That's going to change the rest of their life That's what we're talking about, but the only way we can get the transformation is if we actually sell them something Because all of us say we want to serve, but if we can't sell, we can't serve And to me, live events is one of the best ways we could possibly ever sell somebody on the actual transformation And when I'm talking about people, I'm talking about hundreds of people signing up, hundreds of people celebrating, being welcomed by hundreds of people that just renewed That's transformation, everybody in the room in the chat say transformation And that's what that looks like And to have even more impact, everybody say impact One of my favorite things is by doing these live events, right? We sell a replay, 100% of the replays go to charity, we've been able to build and donate $436,959 directly to charities This is the direct result, again, that if we want to serve, we have to be able to sell There we go Real quick about me, right? Me and my wife, Ronnie, co-founders of Traffic, Sales and Profit International Speakers We published to our company 12 books, 7 full-length documentaries, Ebony Power 100 list, we have Black Enterprise Business Disruptors of the Year We have the 2,000 Club and the 2,000 CX Awards And the very first year they did Bootstrap, we won the Coach Consulting category Thank you Last month, for the fourth year in a row, we won the Inc. 5000 list of the country's fastest growing privately held companies But more importantly, we've sold to over 70,000 customers and clients in all 50 states and 43 countries around the world We sell stuff, we've sold e-books, we've sold courses, we've sold documentaries, workbooks, pop-up events, live events, retreats, four cruises So, through the course of this conversation, I'm talking about things that we've actually done and made happen And Russell said Lamar, can you come out and talk about how you scaled your company through live events? I said I would love to And as we talk about that, there's a few things I want to break down The first is, why in the world should you even do a live event? Because I know people think about it Well Lamar, you know, we can do everything online now And during the pandemic, that greatly accelerated all of that But the first thing I want to talk about is, I love to do live events because they build culture Everybody in the room in the chat say culture And when I talk about building culture I want to break down, I want to talk about 3 C's In your notes, put down 3 C's And when we talk about building culture, right, the first C to culture is content Most of you, when you came into the ecosystem, right, of ClickFunnels and Russell Brunson There was some piece of content that you connected directly with that brought you here In the chat, I want you to tell me what that was It could have been a podcast, it could have been a webinar, it could have been a short reel that you saw on Instagram Or that you saw on YouTube Some piece of content piqued your interest and got you here to make it happen Then once we have content, we got to have consistency Because when we need the content, then we got to start to stack the content on top of each other Because one piece of content is not enough What does that look like? You finally got that video that's been sitting on your phone for 6 months And now you think you're about to be Tim Scooby-Doo clothes Not with one video, you will not Right, so we got to be able to stack the actual content on top of each other Consistency is how we do it Once we have content and we have consistency, the missing piece is creativity That's the special sauce How can we pull together something special that only we do? That special sauce could be the energy that you bring It could be the way you talk, the way you walk What I love in a live event is that we can fill your energy like nobody's business I love it Now here's the bonus These three things build culture But if you add all four together, right? Content, consistency, creativity and culture This is also... This is a terrible arrow There we go How you get to community And community is what live events thrive on My very first funnel hack in live, I think it was 2017 We were in Disney, Coronado Springs Resort Was anybody there, right? Let me know in the chat in the room Everybody from the ClickFunnel team is like, yeah, I was there But I was there and I was sitting with some friends at lunch, right? And one of the guys I was with came and he sat down And as he sat down, he said, Lamar, I just met a guy in line 53-year-old Iron Welder from Alabama I asked him, do you have ClickFunnel software? He said, no Well, I said, well, what are you doing here? He said, I saw an advertisement that said I was just one funnel away You know what that is? That's culture And there's so many pieces of culture A lot of times you don't have a cash flow because you don't have a culture The culture is what gets people to show up time and time again Coming right out of the pandemic, I spoke at a huge marketing event That was one of the biggest in the countries in San Diego And I went out there and guess what? The event was empty There were 7,000 seats and it felt like maybe 700 people were in the room Two weeks later, I come to Florida for Funnel Hacking Live And I'm like, yo, I cannot be in a room like this again And guess what? Funnel Hacking Live was sold out Why? Because of one word, culture, right? So live events allows to build culture They also drive massive engagement Anybody ever not attended an event and you were on the outside looking over the digital fence Hoping that you were there, that formal fear of missing out Looking on social media trying to get any type of nugget that you could from the speakers And pledging that you would never miss another one again Anybody ever missed the FHL and said, hey, you know what? That will never happen again I got to make sure I'm there next time Live events allow you to drive new revenue Also, they are great for a new launch strategy When I want to launch something new into the marketplace Now, purpose of events I'll talk about real quickly because most people have this wrong Most of the conferences and live events you see are education events Meaning you pay the money, right? And all they're going to do is education But the problem is that most events don't educate anybody Anybody ever been to a conference in a room and in a chat and let me know Where you went and then at the end of it, you said, why in the world did I spend my money and my time coming to this? Because I have nothing literally, right? I can't tell you how many times I go to something and I leave with no notes And even worse, I actually have nothing to execute on It could be a fulfillment event. I talked about how we sold a course And in that course, it actually had a ticket to an event And we had to fulfill on that and actually do it But it also was a hybrid event because not only did we fulfill We sold a new program at that event as well So it was fulfillment and sales together We're talking about sales events today And as we talk about it, right, before I get in, I just want to say These are the biggest mistakes I see people consistently make Y'all know what the mistakes are? Let me know in the room in the chat if you do say yes The biggest mistakes I see, right, people do not watch the financials In live events, you have to watch the financials What does that mean? Most people think, well, hey Lamar, I make all my money when I get to the stage and when I pitch What I'm going to tell you is you make all your money when you strategize the stage And when you create your budget in the front Second mistake people make, right, is they don't promote hard enough In my community, I have a rule. I call it no secret sales And secret sales mean you got a promotion, you got an offer But there is no hint or clue about it anywhere Not via your email, not via your text, not via your social media, right I got to get CSI crime scene investigators out there to find your actual offer Mistake people make, they don't create an agenda that leads to the offer They don't start with the end in mind Most people say, well, I'm doing a live event Here's all the content, here are all the speakers And then they say, well Lamar, what should I sell? It's the exact opposite. Somebody said, what? People do that all the time So I want to do just the exact opposite. I want to start with the end in mind What am I going to sell? And once I know what I'm going to sell I say, hey, this is the content I need to create to get to that actual offer And then I say, these are the people that I want to share the actual content to get to the actual offer We start with the end in mind Now, somebody's saying, well Lamar, this is great, but I can't do a live event that would take a miracle What I want to say is you don't need a miracle, you just need a framework And we talk about a framework We're going to talk about PDP And PDP is going to be certain things need to happen pre-event Then you got what happens during the event And then I'm going to give you a few strategies to use post-event Now as I'm going, if you don't get a piece of it, don't worry about it Because the great thing is, before the event, they said Lamar, can we have some type of bonus There were great bonuses that came with this event And one of the bonus I gave you is a master class I only provide to my clients But if you got the bonuses, you have the master class So you can go much deeper than we're about to go, but I want to hit the main points to make sure you have what you need When we talk about what happens pre-event, everybody in the room in the chat say pre It's few things need to happen Number one, you need to say, how many seats do I need to fill? We discuss and we determine how many seats we need to fill based on what the goal is For how many people we want to sell and enroll, we start with the end in mind Then we say, hey, what advertising channels will I use? Bill told us yesterday that every company with common sense advertises He may not say it exactly like that, but it was close, right? Here's a question I got for y'all A little company out of Oregon called Nike, does Nike advertise? Let me know in the room in the chat, does Nike advertise? A company called, you see them, they got like a little smiley face type of thing Amazon, out of Seattle, right? Known all around the world As big as Amazon is, does Jeff Bezos in Amazon do the advertising in the room and in the chat? Another company I'm going to talk about, McDonald's, right? We're not going to get into whether you eat them, whether they're healthy or not That's not what we're talking about today But McDonald's, known around the world, does McDonald's advertise in the room in the chat? So if we know those companies all around the world and they advertise And we all know them, but nobody knows you, why wouldn't you advertise? We got to get deeper, right? So how are we going to advertise? Is it Facebook? Is it Instagram? Is it direct mail, right? Is it some combination of all those different pieces? Then I need to figure out what's my advertising budget? Who can I borrow traffic from? When we talk about borrowing traffic, one of the things I like to do is I like to find groups and organizations That have my avatar They got people that are the demographic and psychographic makeup of who I need in my event In a great place for me, I like to work with local chambers, right? So that's one of the examples So I go to a chamber of commerce and I'll say, hey, you know what? We got a TSP event And I have 20% available that I can give you Now out of that 20%, we can do it a few different ways And I want to find out exactly what they need So for some organizations, right, what they need is money Everybody in the room in the chat say money And with money, they may say, hey, we need money for our budget So what I'll say is, hey, you know what? If you sell us, I'll give you a 20% affiliate commission On everything that you sell Now, some of them may not need money What they actually need is benefits of membership Everybody in the room in the chat say benefits So for these people, right, they say, hey, the budget is good We just need something to let people know that they're in the right place for being a member So we say, hey, we'll give you a 20% discount on all tickets sold Does it matter to us which one they take? No, because both of them equal the same to us Now, some people, they'll say, hey, you know what? We need both, so we'll do a 10% and 10% 10% discount, 10% affiliate, right? Again, at the end of the day, it all means the same thing to us We want to tap into people that already have our people The slowest possible thing you could do is organically try to go out and find people One by one by one We got to accelerate the process Now, when we talk about how do we do this? How do we move on? How do we make it happen? We have to market to fill a room You must be able to express the value that your event provides What is special about it? And what I'm seeing more than ever before coming out of the pandemic Is you must be able to express what is the transformation that they'll have when they get out of here It used to be good enough to say, hey, this is a marketing event, this is a business event Now you have to be able to tell people that when you leave, this is going to be the outcome that you actually get When we talk about filling a room, you have to market hard and heavy from day one I'm going to talk about that in a second And you've got to know your ticket purchase patterns, right? And how they can benefit you and use that to track data When I talk about purchase patterns, this is what I mean For us, we do a pre-sale at the current event The current event is the bliss point That is the opportunity when people are more excited than any other time So we want to make the offer for them when they're more excited than what? Any other time When we make that offer for them at that time, it's going to be the best offer we have And we want to make that offer for people that are currently in attendance And at the same time, email, SMS, get the message out to people that are previous attendees The people that have already attended your event Or the people that are most likely to attend again Does that make sense? So, we do that pre-sale Our event is Thursday, Friday, Saturday The pre-sale starts on that last day of our event on Saturday I announced it at the conference At the conference, I only announced it with one keynote I don't have all the speakers lined up Because I'm not selling them on speakers, I'm selling them on culture Right? And the people that have already attended, they don't need to know all the speakers They just need to know the culture of what we're doing, who we are Because they trust that Lamar and Ronnie are going to put the right people in front of them To help their business grow next year When y'all think about coming to Funnel Hacking Live When I come to Funnel Hacking Live, I'm not thinking about who wrestling the team are lining up for speakers I'm just saying that I got to get in position and get in the building Because I know my transformation is where? In the building So I just got to get there So on that day, we have one main keynote We announced the keynote, we opened up ticket sales That pre-sale for current and previous attendees goes from that Saturday The last day of our event, Saturday to the next Friday A little bit less than a week Then after that, we go dark for two weeks For two weeks, we don't do anything, we build up pressure We have a waiting list, the early bird tickets are coming out Then we release early bird tickets after two weeks on the following Monday 9am Eastern time, we open them up After early bird, we do a sell price After sell price, we do a full price Then we have to just determine what's going to be the price increase And what's going to be the length of time between each Our events are six months apart, so we pretty much do them like once every month, month and a half at the tops The reason we do them Russell talked about this this morning When he was talking about prime movers He said, I'm taking the offer off the table Because I know if I don't, most of you will never make a decision And it's marketers, right? It is our job, I want y'all to get this It is our job that you have to get people to make a decision It's not that most people don't want what you have to offer They just don't want to make a decision And guess what? Making no decision is actually a decision So it's our job to force them off defense So how our ticket sales go is that when we do pre-sale We sell, right, 200 or 300 tickets right there in that block Because those are people most likely to come They don't need a lot of nudge When we do early bird, right, we first set it Those are the early adapters they jump in Majority of the other tickets are sold, guess when? Right before the price goes up So we got to say, hey, this we doing a market This we doing a fill of room, this we doing to make it happen Y'all getting value, you getting value from this Say yes in the room, say yes in the chat I want to make sure we're on pace Now, this we do during the event Everybody in the room in the chat say during During the event, right, we create the energy and the experience It's impossible to have a transformation where there is no energy So we want to create that, how do we do that One of the main ways we do it, I did a presentation To the Atlas group around how we use music How we use video, how we use media, right I got a DJ, but the DJ's just not playing DJ stuff I go to the DJ every morning, we talk about What is the way we want that room to feel What is the energy that we want in that room, right That's the conversation we have, that's the strategy Months before the event, right now we're working on our January event I got a meeting next week with my video team We're going to sit down because every day we open a conference with a video Not to recap, we show the recap But before then we open with a video to set the stage For what we want the emotion to be that day Sometimes it's like, hey, we want people to feel like they're part of the community Sometimes it's like, hey, we want people so angry that they're ready to take action for their lives Sometimes it's like, hey, you know what We want people to appreciate that guess what, they finally found a home And this is the home for them So we strategize like, what is the emotion we want for them every day Then we have social proof in the event of the system success One of the great things about this event, have the speakers been amazing, have they been amazing But one of the great things about the event is that a lot of the speakers are people that are either in Russell's programs Or were in the programs previously at some point So there's social validation that the program work if you work the program So how can you implement your own people into your actual event, right? 90% of our speakers are people that are in the programs Now they got to know what they're talking about They subject matter experts, let me add that part, right? Y'all be like, well Lamar told me to put Miss Suzie on there And Miss Suzie can half way talk about what in the world she's talking about, right? They got to be subject matter experts, we want to get our own people in Then I'm not a big fan of panels, but I have two panels A six-figure panel and a seven-figure panel So I want to get as many potential people in front of them as possible Because you have to realize no matter how big your ego is There are people in the audience that will never connect with you And the more successful you become, the more they make excuses about your success Well hey, you know what? You're great and that's cool But it's easy for you because you have a team Somebody told me that time, one time y'all They said Lamar, it's easy for you because you got 40 people on the team I said what was easy was when it was just me And I didn't carry that load of 40 families on my back Y'all about to take me down another path, let me get my clicker back out They hit a chord in me, right? My mind went somewhere Alright, right? But they'll make all these excuses about your success And why you're successful and they are not So we got to get real focused on, hey, you know what? I want to put as many people in front of them as possible So they can find someone they connect with I want a woman, I want a man, I want somebody old, I want somebody young I want a product base, I want a service base Somebody they can connect with that has a connection with me and the program In the event, during the event, everything that we do has to lead to what? The offer, right? And we do that too, the big things I want to talk about is Hey, we have to upgrade the identity, Russell talked about this yesterday And we also have to challenge their beliefs, there was some conversation about that as well When we talk about identity, right? They talked about it yesterday, Salinas mentioned one part Not just the internal identity, and I loved it When Russell talked about it, he was talking about the identity of click funnels More than any other community, you know what that identity is People would say for years, I am a what? Funnel hacker, let me see it in the chat Yesterday, Russell said, hey, guess what? The identity has changed, now I am a what? Prime mover, right? Where are the new prime movers in the chat? If you are a prime mover in the chat, put PM in the chat Y'all, let's give it up one more time, let's celebrate the new prime movers that have joined the family This beginning of a journey is going to change the rest of your life But also with the identity, what is the outside transformation of that? They may have came to the journal admission of VIP, but we want to cut that wristlet off As soon as you show us, hey, guess what? The positive is in, come to the table, we want to take that off We want to put a new wristlet on you, this is your now part of the mastermind This is going to give you access to new opportunities, new places inside the event But more importantly, new people Because people that invest like to connect with other people that invest That is an immediate signal, when you join prime movers, the other people that join said that's an immediate signal That person has made a sizable investment in their life and in their business So they'll want to meet Also, when we replace the lanyard, right? We just don't take one lane and give them another one We crown them with the lanyard Too many times we ask people to make a hot ticket investment, but we give them a low ticket experience Let me know if y'all got that on the chat Chicken nugget, there we go, I'm working on a six piece up here I got me sweating A 12, there we go, appreciate that, appreciate that So how can we have both a hot ticket program and a hot ticket experience That begins not when they start or when they log in, but as soon as they make the actual exchange of currency That's what we do, another way we do that, right? We want to have some physical item that we give away even though they made a digital purchase How many times, let me know in the chat in the room, have you bought a five, ten thousand dollar course and all you got was an email in the log in And you're like, hey, you know what, this is what I paid for, but y'all, especially, I'm a lot older than I look I'm like Benjamin Button up here But, back in the days when you bought something, you walked away with something Anybody remember that? Nowadays, like you buy something and you get the email and you're like, you know, like, is something else come with this? So we like to give them a physical item, we give them one of these boxes And you know how they walk around with the boxes, they walk around like this They cherish the box, they love the box, because to them the box is not just a piece of swag or an item To them the box is a twenty five, thirty, fifty thousand dollar investment in the business And at this stage, for most of us, the investment in the business is really an investment in ourselves So we like to do that When I talk about belief system, right, we have to challenge their beliefs But one of the things I like to share is that most of us, the way we believe comes from our environment Everybody in the room in the chat say environment And when we talk about our environment, here's the thing, right How we were raised, the people that we were surrounded by, family, friends, greatly influences But even if we had great people immediately around us, every day from the day of birth we are bombarded by the world And here's the problem, I don't like doing what the world tells me to do, because I don't want what most of the people in the world actually have Most of the people in the world do not have happiness, they do not have time freedom, they do not have financial freedom So why in the world would I listen to those folks? And here's what the world tells us, right, when we talk about beliefs It's something I like to call have, and on the other side the world says it's things that we should hope for And we talk about having hope, they say, hey, you know, you should have the big house that you dream about You should have your dream car, they say you should have the clothes, hope there's no travel agency that hate this part You should have the trips, right, put the trips on Instagram, put it on the gram, show them what you're doing And here's the thing, they say no matter, right, these are great things to aspire towards But the problem is they say no matter what you've done or not done, you deserve to have it and people will never question you If you've got four clothes on the last house and you just got kicked out of your apartment because you couldn't afford rent And you'd lock up in a new house, everyone wants to know when the house warming and when you're having your first cook out If your car just got repossessed and you get another car, and this car you're upside down on Your payments are two times what they should be, all people care about is can you pick them up? The next time we go out you're driving, can I take a selfie in front of your car? When you got the clothes, they want to celebrate that, when you got the trips, they want to say hey, when you go on the next time Even though they know they're not coming with you, when you go on the next time, right, we want to get into it These are all the things the world says we should have, on the other side, they say these are the things we should hope for We hope that one day we can build a business We hope that one day my business can have a team so I don't have to do all of it alone We hope that one day, right, I can run ads like Bill, Bill said he's running $150k a day Well I hope that one day I can just run $1,500 or maybe even $150k They say hey I hope that, you know besides the business team that adds one day I can have a coach Right, I want to be a prime mover, one day I'm going to be a prime mover, right I'm going to implement what they talked about and I'm going to do that Now here's the thing, because the world says that we should have all of these things The world will look at you crazy when you tell them that you want to have this Anybody ever told somebody they were launching a business and they looked at you like you were crazy Why would you launch a business when you have all of those college degrees? You're still paying the student loans or those? Why would you launch a business when you have your dream job? What if your dream job ain't your dream is theirs? People say well you know what, why you want a team, right, they say it's pee in the hiring pool right now Why would you want to have a team? They're not going to do what you want to People don't work like they used to, like when you run ads you're just giving money to Zuckerberg Why would you want a coach, right? This is what they say, like if you get a coach It's all about the coach making money, not you It's a scam, it's multi-level marketing, anybody ever heard any of that in the room or in the chat? The world will look at you crazy if you try to get the business team to add the coach But here's what I realized, again, when the world tells me to go right I'm going to do what? Go left, when the world tells me to go up, what am I going to do in the chat? I'm going to go down So when the world tells me these are the things I should have, what I realize is that if I get the house to call to close the trips They don't help me get a business team of ads or a coach But if I flip these to a rail and I say I deserve to have a business In my business I deserve to have a team so I ain't got to do all the work by myself In my business I deserve to advertise because more people need to know about my product or service In my business I deserve to have a coach, I deserve to be a prime mover Because why on earth would I want to figure out everything myself the hard way? If I say I deserve to have these things The business team that adds the coach can get me the house to call to close the trips But it doesn't work the other way around We got to start thinking different than what everybody tells us The first year that me and Ronnie worked together, right? I said we work together in the business, we're complete opposites The first year that me and Ronnie worked together in the business It was tax time, we went to our CPA Now I'm a risk taker, any risk takers in the chatter in the room, let me see by show of hands, let me see by comment Any conservative people, right, savers, the thrifters, you... Ronnie ain't got a hand up, right, there's at least one in the audience Okay there we go, we have another thing, it's two people that admit it, right? If you're conservative that's cool too, right, so we're opposites So during that time I basically saved nothing for retirement, right? I was like, because why would I need retirement? I'm going to be rich My first 20 businesses failed, right, but 21 is it, that's how entrepreneurs think At the same time Ronnie had always saved, right, I see the savers in the chat, right I see you, Sorai, I see you, Jewel So Ronnie had always saved, so went to the CPA and Ronnie said it doesn't make me feel good Because this is the first year ever, right, because all of our money went to the business That I haven't put money into my retirement I haven't put money into savings And checked this out, here is what the CPA told us And it blew my mind forever The CPA looked at Ronnie, the CPA looked at me And he said, why do you think it's more important to invest in somebody else's business versus your own? Somebody caught that Because what we realized is that we put more weight on investing into Elon Musk and Tesla Than we did investing into Tyler New Media, our own company And so many times we'll easily invest 10, 20, 30 grand into Apple, into Nvidia Into all these other companies, right, on the markets But when you talk about investing in yourself, even you think that you're crazy for doing that At some point we have to put more value in ourselves than we do outside entities That's what that looks like And that's the way we even think, right, before we can impact the clients and the customers Real quick, post-event, right, these are a few things we do, make sure you write them down They are big We do after the event, and in case you missed it, webinar So the event I told you is Thursday, Friday, Saturday, it ends on Saturday That next Tuesday, night, 8 p.m. Eastern time, right, so people are freeing at home I do it in case you missed it And it's for two sets of people If you came to the conference, you should have made a decision, but you didn't, right You might have had to talk to somebody, you may have to move money, we'll give you one last chance You had a question, it didn't get answered, we'll give you one last chance And if you didn't come to the conference, but you heard that we offered and we opened up our program And you want more information, this is your chance to do it Only in case you missed it, webinar I am not teaching, I am not training, the only thing I'm doing is what? Somebody tell me in the chat, sell it I get on, and the only thing I'm talking about is again, this is the value stack This is what we offered, this is the actual price, here to frequently ask questions And hey, I take Q&A for as long as they got them We always, 100% of the time close more people when we do that extra webinar call Now, thank you Now we issue a survey to attendees, on that survey is two ways you can phrase it, two things you can do One, you can say, hey, you attended the event, would you be interested in learning more about working with our team? The other thing you can do is you can say, hey, you attended the event, but we notice if you segment your attendees We notice you didn't join the program that we offered when we actually opened it up What is their reason why? They respond back and tell you the reason why, now you know what the objection is And you can have somebody try to challenge that objection Even if you can't get them to purchase, now you know what's in the mindset of the people So you can address that in the next event as you do it, does that make sense? Right, once we get the replays back, then we can do a recap event Now the recap event is going to show highlights of some of the best speakers, right? The best moments, but also one of the best moments is when I opened up my program So we have to include that, now when I include that, I'm not going to give them the same offer that I did at the actual event Because at the event I said the offer was special, so guess what, it's going to stay special We have to have integrity around the events and the things that we actually offer After that we do follow-up calls to the team, we wrap up, collect the budget And we have meetings with key team members to make sure they have what they need And to make sure the things that went well, we keep in it, the things that didn't go well, we get rid of Let me know, I see a lot of nuggets, Michael, Diane, thank you We're coming through, Joanne said gold nugget, I appreciate it Now, I'm going to do a quick case study, TSB Live, our conference 2022, our biggest enrollment The one I showed pictures from, 867 total attendees 217 of those people are current clients, so they couldn't buy from us, new, right? And I feel like God spoke to me and he was like, I want you to give these companies away I was like, no, this is Satan, rebuke you Never, not today We had just gotten professionally evaluated for $78 million, so it was a big deal And he was like, give him away, and I said, I'll make a deal with you If you tell my wife I'll do it And if not, you're the devil So we ain't doing it Anybody relate with this? Because hearing from God ain't that hard Obeying God is very different So I get home to the Airbnb that we're staying at And are you good? You having fun? Good, I love you Ben I get back to the Airbnb and I say, hey Lindsay, I think God might have spoken to me, but probably not Caby, I feel like he told me to give the businesses away Completely And she begins to cry And I was like, yes, I need this woman, God And I said, well, it's okay, it's okay, we don't have to, come here, let me comfort you and whatever She said, no, no, no, God told me that you were going to have to do this two weeks ago And I said, I'm not telling him Anybody have to make a deal with God to backfires? I said, okay And the key, the trick to obedience, let me tell you this real fast I'm going to teach you something, it'll change everything about your life forever The key to obedience or power, regardless of whether you believe in it or not Is you have to obey before you can talk yourself out of obey Like, it's like getting in a cold plunge Where you do not want to get in the cold plunge But if you can just get in before your brain realizes that you're killing yourself in a cold plunge Then you're good And so I just fired off an email Some of you are feeling this way right now Like, this is what I need to do, but rationally, oh, no, no, no You just do it before you can talk yourself out of it And your life becomes fruitful as you obey Does this make sense, yes or no? If I had to predict where I would be one year from the day I signed up to join Russell's Mastermind, I could never have predicted the growth. It has been a life-changing experience. Funnel Hiking Live is an event that is put on by Russell Brunson, where he shares with internet entrepreneurs exactly how to accelerate their success by using a software that he created called ClickFunnels to create websites that actually make sales in a very easy way for people who are looking to succeed online. ClickFunnels came out of nowhere. They took the marketplace by storm, just like Apple did when they first launched the iPhone. It was not a linear move anymore. It was a quantum move, an improvement that opened up a gap to allow people just like you and me, who are not technically savvy. We weren't born in the matrix and we didn't have a chip implanted in our heads. We were not engineers or computer science majors. We were science majors. An opportunity to cut and decrease the time of stress and anxiety and suffering behind a keyboard. You can talk bad about my mama, but you cannot talk bad about ClickFunnels because what ClickFunnels has done for my business, I can't even begin to tell you. Russell just makes it so simple for anybody to just instantly get it. He's so authentic. One of the most genuinely nice individuals and human beings you will ever meet. Breaks it down really easy. He's just a risk taker. He's a mover and a shaker. The first time I spoke with him, you know, immediately I knew I was playing small. We took these guys to an exotic car racing place. What's better than getting an exotic car? It's called dream racing, right? Like all it is is a dream in some people's mind. Being able to go to the dream racing track, the exotic track, and get into a Lamborghini and get into an Aston Martin. And what it seats is you tell it either it's going to be a dream or it's going to be a reality. It's really up to you. Russell was running a contest. He was going to give his Ferrari F430. All his idea was tell the world about this awesome book and I got this Ferrari, which is absolutely ridiculous. It instills in you that necessity to really give to other people. I think we're over $25,000 right now for world teacher aid. Every single funnel that gets created, a dollar gets donated to world teacher aid. I think Russell was able to raise money for two schools and that's huge. They were all excited to be there and excited to be in that room. Being an entrepreneur, I would say it's like the loneliest job in the world because it's kind of you and your thoughts and you see other people and you kind of connect and not like in a real way with them. I like bringing people together. It just gives you the ability to be around other people who are like you, who think like you and who believe like you. And for me, every time I get around good entrepreneurs, my vision gets bigger. You literally sit next to people who are doing a million dollars. Other people who are just brand new. Everyone who's here, they want to share and they want to see everyone else succeed. I come to a live event where Russell can guide me, where people next to me are smarter than me. And I share information that's absolutely priceless. I wanted real ClickFunnels members to come in to speak. This funnel type of stuff, this is what I did, how I did it, how I'm making 100 grand a month. And all of a sudden it like becomes real. You get to hear more stories and more action plans and more principles to business. In the trenches, what's working now, information. You want results faster? Here's a faster hack. Go this way. Here's the concept and then boom, here's it. Here's it working for someone just like you. And that was, that was my favorite part. It will accelerate your success like nothing else out there. ClickFunnels There's just been so many tools out there. Very few have been aggregated to do everything you need to do to create success. ClickFunnels is an amazing software that allows you to sell a product online quicker and faster than anything you've ever seen in your life. It's just a simple way of making pages that convert, you know, I mean they're like business oriented templates that already start beautiful, but that you can very easily customize. It's the same with text an hour or two, it takes three hours and I have a whole funnel belt. And then I just, I should go. You know in one day, you can get that idea, you can be live. You can take that idea out of your head and you can actually make it real. Author speakers, high-end coaching. We do marketing products for real estate agents. T-shirts. Every kind of person is here and it's awesome. Whenever we think about hacks, tips and tricks, right, it's like the fast way to do it. It's about taking best practices from people who have already proven what works. Funnel hacking isn't about stealing, it's about modeling and creating your own spin on something that's already working. I think entrepreneurship is being hacked here. It's people who are doing stuff. Every single session that I've been in has pretty much been a standing ovation. So many of us as entrepreneurs have limiting beliefs. We think money is something we should be afraid of. We should be afraid to lose it. We should be afraid to ask for it. What is being talked about here is how to overcome that. What do you want? And why are you here? I came to Funnel Hacking Live without a business. I was working two jobs. I still didn't have enough. I procrastinated my way into depression. I'd just gotten out of a heavy addiction. I was sued by multiple creditors. This is not my life. This is not my life. I know I'm meant for more. You find yourself lost, frustrated, disconnected. Because you have no fire. Put your seat belts on. When you go, you don't know what you need. You don't really know what you're going to find. All the way from being a contractor to standing on ClickFunnels page? This is crazy. Bro, good to see you. By the way, I just did $12 million with this guy about six months ago. Unbelievable. Bill Allen. Oh my gosh. Okay. My name is Pace Morby. I'm sure most of you guys don't know me. I am a real estate guy. I'm the guy in the real estate industry that has changed the way people do real estate, but it's funny that they're not having me talk to you guys about real estate. They're having me talk about building a community. I started out as a contractor. Why did I start out as a contractor? Because my father taught me how to be a contractor. I grew up in a family of 12 children, and my parents would wake us up every morning at 5 a.m. We'd read the Bible, we'd go around, read a verse together, and then my mom would always ask, what was your favorite thing of this morning? And my mom would say, my mom was great, by the way. Imagine a woman raising 12 kids. Woo. And my mom would say, what are you guys going to go do today? And I felt community my entire life. I felt connected to people my entire life. And it wasn't until I left my mom's home, my parents' home, that I saw that the regular world was not like this, especially the construction industry. Very backstabby, very cutthroat, and I thought there was something wrong with me. I thought there was something wrong with me. I was always trying to get people to become my friends. I would DM other contractors, I'd say, hey, let's go play golf, let's go hang out together. And they'd say, what are you trying to steal from me? What's wrong with you? And that's how it felt when I jumped into construction, and then ultimately I was touching all these houses, right? Me and my construction crews, we would touch all these houses, we'd flip all these houses for other people, and finally, when I was about 23 years old, I had this feeling I need to get into real estate. But guess what? I read every book, I watched every video, I watched every YouTube channel you could imagine, and just me being a contractor, do you guys think I learned how to frame a house by watching it on YouTube? Do you think I learned how to pour concrete foundation by watching it in somebody's course, in their videos, in their modules on Kajabi? Is that where I learned how to do that? How did I learn how to do it? Somebody stood next to me and showed me how to do it. That's how I became a contractor. And so for seven years, I kept telling myself, I'm going to get into real estate, I'm going to get into real estate, I'm going to get into real estate, and I did all the things. I bought all the courses, I paid all the things, and guess what? I'm the reason why gurus get a bad name, because I'm the guy that buys the course and doesn't do anything with it. Well, luckily, I had somebody change that for me. It was a woman, her name was Bethany. I won't go into the story of my first real estate transaction. But what happened is I started getting into real estate, and I learned some really magical things. Today, I have $500 million of real estate in my portfolio. I've never once used my credit. I've never used a bank, and I've never used any of my own money. Isn't it crazy that they're not having me talk about that today? It's because as obsessed I am about real estate, I'm actually ten times more obsessed about what I'm going to be talking to you guys today about, which is building a community and changing your entire industry. Well, here's what happened in my real estate business. So I got into the business. Bethany was this amazing mentor for me. It was very much physical. We would hang out with each other. I would do deals with her. She'd buy deals from me, and she walked me through every transaction for years. And then one day Bethany retires, and I lost that physical touch, that community, that aspect that I needed, the human being. Well, one day, I get a lead. This is how real estate works for us. I'm not a real estate agent, nor would I ever be. I'm not smart enough to be a real estate agent. I'm a real estate investor. We would send out postcards to people and say, anybody ever get those postcards? We'll buy your house for cash? I'm very sorry, that's me. Or somebody I taught how to do that. So we send out these postcards. I get this lead. I'm in Phoenix, Arizona, buying real estate in Phoenix, but I get a lead from a guy up in Denver, Colorado. And he says, hey, Pace, just got your postcard. I want to sell my house in Phoenix. I live in Denver. Great, we go back and forth and we strike a price. This is Wednesday morning, we strike a price over the phone. He says, perfect. When can I see you in person? I'd like to sign the contract. I go, oh, no problem. I'll send over, I'll just send over a docuSign. Everybody know what docuSign is? He says, am I doing a real estate deal with docuSign, or am I doing a real estate deal with you? I was like, oh, I'm so sorry, I'll send over a mobile notary. Do you guys know what a mobile notary is? He says, am I doing the deal with a mobile notary? I guess not. Who do you need to meet you? He says, I'm an old school guy. I want to do a deal with somebody that I'm selling the house to, eyeball to eyeball. You have till Friday at 5 p.m. to come to my house in person and sign this contract, or else you're not the man I want to sell this house to. Dang it. All right. So what happens is I go on what would be called a community. I go on a page. I go on a forum, and I say, I need some help. This is about eight years ago. I go, I need some help. I've got a lead up in Colorado. I need somebody to be my joint venture partner on this real estate transaction. I'll split it with you 50-50. I post it on there. The first thing that happened, I just started getting ridiculed by other people that have a real estate license. I don't have a real estate license. Criticism, criticism, criticism, criticism, criticism. I'm like, oh my gosh. All right. This is not worth it. And I'm trying to think like, okay, who do I know in Denver, Colorado, that can help me get this real estate transaction done? More importantly, who do I know I can trust? And actually knows how to talk to the seller about the thing. Well, I just kind of was willing to give up this Wednesday night and I get a text message from a gentleman named Steve. I tagged Scott and I tagged Tim. I have 3,000 followers. Everybody in here probably has more than 3,000 followers. So what happened is the following week, how many people showed up? 63 people showed up. Brand is what people say behind your back. The number one algorithm, the number one algorithm you've got to care about is the spoken word from a human being's mouth. That's the number one algorithm. Everybody else is trying to get downloads and this and that. The number one thing is your reputation. The speed of sound, my friends. Nothing is faster than the speed of sound. People's mouth talking about what you do. So 63 people showed up to Circle K. And I'm doing the thing. I'm like, oh man, now I can't handle this. This is too many freaking people. He's like, it's Saturday at 6 a.m., bro. Get an office. I do have an office. So I started doing meet-ups. This is what I did. I had no idea. There was no book. And I started doing meet-ups and I go, all right, I'm going to buy the biggest whiteboard I could buy. I didn't know what a funnel was, a coaching product was. I didn't know any of this stuff. Nothing. And I go, all right, I'm going to order a 26-foot whiteboard and I'm going to sit there every Saturday and answer every question until that whiteboard is fully filled up. Guys, this got so big, so, so big that our parking lot didn't have space. And all of a sudden, we got a line of people wanting to take a picture with me. A freaking contractor that drives a Prius. What do you want a photo with me for? And there's this guy, he's kind of being weird. He's like, definitely a European. No offense to you Europeans, but he's just kind of moving a little bit different, you know? Comes all the way through. Comes all the way through. Comes all the way through the line. And I go, bro, what's up, man? Like, did you have seven Red Bulls? He goes, no, I'm just so excited to meet you. I go, cool, man. What part of the valley do you live in? I go, cool, where are you from? He goes, I'm from Portugal. I go, cool, how long have you been in the United States? He goes, I've been in the United States for 45 minutes.
