Now, this is me. Remember, I'm going to get really strong here. I'm going to get really nasty. There's going to be some coaches in here. They're going to DM me and go, I hate you. You're horrible. I'm just going to tell you what I do. Russell mistakenly gave me 60 minutes. So I get to talk some smack. So this got out of control. What did I have to do? I responded. Nobody ever told me to go be a coach. I responded. I go, how do I handle all these questions? People are now texting me throughout the week. I don't know how to respond. So I create a free Facebook group. That free Facebook group today has 165,000 people in it. I create this free Facebook group and all of a sudden it goes from 40 people to 100 people to 4,000 people. And in my first week, it was like maybe 11,000 people and it just caught wildfire until we started filtering it like crazy. Now you have to like answer every question in the book. The Facebook group went crazy. And what I did is I go, man, now I have my names on this thing. So I would wake up in the morning. My wife just barely had a baby. So I'd wake up in the morning. I go out in the garage and I'd pull up in my laptop and I would answer every single question with a video response from 3am to 6am. Are you going to do that? Probably not. I also personally still answer all my DMs. I get over 1100 DMs a day. But what are the non scalable items that nobody else is willing to do? Now I didn't sit down there. I'm not Alex Hermosi where I'm like planning out how to be strategically brilliant at marketing. I just showed up the way I wish somebody else showed up for me. And what happened is it got so crazy. I started having people ask me to speak. Pace, will you come and speak about what you're doing? I go, sure. Like, where, like in Scottsdale? I'm over in Mesa. You want me to come over to Scottsdale? I go, no. Will you come over to Boston? We'll pay for your flight. I'm like, sure. If I can do a meet up, let's do a meet up. I started doing meet ups all over the country in this built demand. And one day a guy named Josiah Grimes comes to me. Who knows Josiah Grimes? My business partner, him and I own a bunch of real estate together. We've been doing real estate. We were doing real estate deals together six years prior to this. And he comes to me and goes, I've never seen anybody care about people so much. You need to be a coach. What's my response? Coach. That sounds gross. I never want to be a coach. I never want to be a mentor. Why? A couple of reasons. Because people in my industry, the real estate industry, take people, take advantage of people. That was one of the biggest things. They just sell a course. Here's the information. Here's a once a week thing. And in six months you're out. And they would call that a community. Are you kidding me? I grew up in a church that if you did something wrong, something bad happened to you. That pastor, that bishop was still there two years later waiting for you to come back. That was a community to me. You're not a member of this church because you're perfect. You're not a member of this church only temporarily as long as you keep paying your tithing. You are a member of this church because you are one of God's children. And so I would watch this and I would watch how people treated their community as a pincushion. And when Josiah came to me, he goes, I want you to go launch a coaching product. I think that you could change the world. I said, no, there's no possible way you're getting me to do that. And he says, well, what do you want to do? Where do you think this is going? I said, well, I have these voice memos like maybe eight months ago I was driving back in my Prius and I have these voice memos. Will you listen to these voice memos and tell me if I'm crazy? I want to go build a community that can change the industry. I want to build a community that changes the industry. And I'll get into what that means here in just a minute. But he sends, I send these voice memos over to me, he goes, that's impossible. He says to me, that's impossible. It's never been done. Nobody's ever done it before. And I go, perfect. Then don't ever call me ever again. Either we're doing it my way or we're not doing it at all. And he comes back to me three months later as I continue to compound and these meetups start going crazy. My Facebook group is growing, blowing up. I don't have a YouTube channel. I don't have my Instagram still like 6000 people, but my Facebook group was like 30,000 people. And he goes, dude, this thing is just there's so many people that need specific guided direction. They need you to build a course. And I go, Nope, I'm not going to build a course. I'm going to build something else. If you're with me, let's do it. And he goes, Okay, fine. What are your rules? And so I want to tell you guys what my rules were when I built my community. Okay, some of you guys are going to like them. The first thing I told them is I said, education is dead. Community is the future. Do we all agree that chat GBT and YouTube and all these things kind of take people's time away. Remember when COVID took over and all we wanted to do is be next to each other. Here's what I did during COVID. I jumped in an air stream and I drove around the country and I met every single one of my students in person. My wife took my kids. And I started right there. I said, I never want to call anybody in our community a student. I don't want to be called a student. I don't want to call them a student. He was, What do you want to call him? I said, I want to call him a member. They're a member of something and we are building something great together. He was, What are we building? And I said, Here's what we're going to need. I want to know what are we solving as a community? What are we solving? If you're selling education, great, go do that. That's not my model. If you want to emulate something I'm doing this, these are my rules. What are we solving was number one. What are we solving? Trying to build a funnel and make more money? I've, Hacob, have I ever looked at our funnel one time? Your funnel is important. I think, but not as important what I'm about to tell you. The thing that I said is I said, We have to solve bigger problems than education. We have to move an entire industry and people need to be a part of that movement. Now that sounds very generic and like, whatever. I'm going to get into some specific stuff in the real estate world. Number one, backstabbing. Two, lack of true go-givers. C, lack of local leadership. And D, lack of quality services with a standard process. And E, new ways of doing business. I said, If we're not doing those things, we're not doing anything at all. Like they can go to YouTube then go somewhere else. I don't want to do that. I want to build something that changes the industry. He goes, Okay, I really don't know what that really means. And I said, Here's what a community should be. A community should be a filtering fee. It's not a membership cost. It is a filtering fee. You guys want to hang out at Costco or at Walmart? And if Europeans are like, What's Costco? We want to hang out at Costco. I want the Costco thing. I want the filtering fee. I want to hang out with the higher level people. That's this group right here. I go to lunch. Literally today, I meet like three of my heroes today at lunch here at this event. Because it's a higher level person. I said, The only reason I want to charge money is because I need a filtering fee to make sure that people are committed to the cause. And I said, The second rule I want to do, Okay, rule number one is what are we solving? Rule number two is ditch digital get personal. I said, Every single local geographical geographical location around the country has to have a physical meetup. Imagine if you guys have a coaching product, imagine somebody joins your coaching product and then they had local support. They could meet in person. That's what we built. We built one. We have 400 meetups every single month across the country. Every month. Chino, where are you at brother? You in here? Chino and Thomas right here. They run the Vegas portion right here. And by the way, guess who bought my Prius from me? Chino Monterey. They're bought that Prius. And now what's cool about Chino Chino, when you joined the community, you took leadership as a serious role and you're leading our Vegas community. You now drive people around in that Prius and you take people who are homeless to the houses that you've bought with no cash, no credit, no credentials. You're using that Prius because you've been passed the torch, correct? So I wanted to solve bigger problems and I wanted to pass a torch. So I wanted to ditch the digital and get personal. Now guys, we're lunatics. Some of you guys are doing one weekly call a week. We do 39 zooms a week. 14 hours a day of live support, six days a week. You ain't going to do it. You won't do it. How do I do that? I built local leadership all over the country that are dynamically good at specific topics. And I never made it about myself. So if you look at my Zoom schedule, you'll see 39 zooms. I do one maybe two a week. My community handles the other 37 to 38. And they're all very, very different. So if I'm a community member, I go, man, I want to learn how to look at a piece of real estate, but PACE isn't available and I don't want to watch the modules. Your students don't want to watch modules. Am I wrong? And so we built a local leadership around the country so that when they ran into a problem, somebody was there physically to either go on an appointment or help them out immediately. Now this is hard. This is really hard to do. You know what I had to do to do this? Travel around the country and find the chinos and build them up and love on them and breathe life into them and give them confidence to believe in themselves to be able to lead a local group of people. And now he makes millions of dollars by doing deals with people locally here. Now that's real estate. Real estate is kind of magical. He can run a local chapter here and he can make a lot of money by helping people in the local chapter. So here's how I did that. I want you guys to write this one down. This was very, very important for everybody. And I don't know what your map looks like, but I'll kind of show you what my map looks like. This is my next rule. I said, I want to map out every single way somebody can make money in my industry. Okay, so let's talk about it for just a second. I'll give you guys some ideas. I don't know your individual industry, but let's say I'm in the beauty industry. Somebody makes the makeup. Somebody applies the makeup. Somebody wholesale the makeup. Packages the makeup. In my industry, somebody talks to the seller. Somebody handles the paperwork, the title and escrow process. Somebody raises the capital. There's so many different ways to make money. Do you know how many people make money in one real estate transaction? On average, it's 51 people make money on one real estate deal. It is crazy. So I sat down and I mapped out every single person on a chart. And I actually think I have a slide for it. My team sometimes gives me slides. By the way, here's what those meetups look like. Two days ago, this was actually in Glacier National Park. I got 450 people to go to the top of Glacier National Park two days ago. Crazy. Okay. I won't talk about that today. Cool. So what I did is I created a map and I called these people, these individual people, avatars. And I made it so instead of me just talking to one person. A lot of you guys are talking to one person, but what about this? Let's think about this for a second. If I have a real estate mentorship and I'm teaching people how to buy and sell real estate, are they not going to interact with a title rep? Are they not going to interact with a private money lender? Are they not going to interact with maybe a contractor? Are they not going to interact with a lot of other people that make money on that same transaction? So what I did is I started building out avatar trainings. I have 26 avatars in my community. And what that does is it makes it so you can join our community. This is what happened in the first week. First week of us launching the community in 2020. We had a girl come into a Zoom. Her name is Kelly Hano. And Kelly Hano says, Pace, you keep saying that this is a community and I'm just blown away. I got my first deal from somebody else in the community. They locked up the contract. I bought it. Somebody brought me my money I needed for the deal in the community. I got the contractor for the house in the community, the property manager in the community, and I got my first tenant from somebody in the community. I said, that is how you build a community. You don't focus on one avatar. You focus on the entire industry and you figure out how to bring those people in. This is the craziest statistic I'll ever give you. We do $100 million a year in revenue in our funnel. I don't even use the word funnel. Some people are like, how do I do a million dollars a year and you're hacking your funnel and you're doing all this stuff? I've never looked at my funnel and would do $100 million. Because of this. 20% of the people, I'll call them students. I don't call them students. I call them members. But 20% of the students that join actually want to do what I do. Stop and think about that for a second. The other 80% already know they don't want to do what I do and they're still joining my community. 80% why? Because 20% of them want to buy deals from those people doing what I do. 20% of them want to lend money to people in my community. They're lenders. 20% of them want to provide other services and 20% of them say I'm not ready to be PACE or ready to be CHINO. I want to join their team. And we created a safe space for all these individual avatars. And so what I would do with you guys is sit down when you go home and go, I'm going to map out how everybody in my industry makes money, A to Z. And I would call those avatars. Now I have an avatar training. I actually told Russell, I'm so obsessed with this topic. I told Russell, I said anybody that's in Prime Mover, let me come in for hours. I will break down every avatar. I'll show you guys how to do it. Anybody have an industry that you are in, you're like, I don't even know what that would be. I don't know how to build an avatar series. Anybody in here that wants me to pick you apart? What do you got? What industry are you in? Okay, holistic wellness. Holistic wellness is awesome. People go in, they actually hire somebody to help them heal. What are all the people that are in that line? People that provide products and services, or products and people that provide services. You have people that do the web pages and all that kind of stuff. We have people that literally join our community just to build real estate websites for the other students. If they aren't in there, then it's not a community. It's just one group of people. Do you see what I'm saying? And so what happens is as my students get more and more successful, what I don't want them to do is have to leave my community to go get a product or a service somewhere else. I want to bring that all in one place, one ecosphere. Does that make sense? Okay. Now, here are some things that I'm doing to actually change the industry. Last year, now this is going to be tough. I don't know what these problems are in your industry. You're going to have to figure out what these problems are in your industry. If I was, maybe if you were in Prime Mover, I could sit down with you in one of these zooms that Russell's going to do and I could help you out. But what I did is I looked at my industry and I go, okay, where are my students spending their money? If they come in, they go buy a house, they take a contract, and they take it to a title company. That title company makes a lot of money. I sat down and I found that Fidelity Title, big company, they made $100 million in profit from my community over the last four years. Where is that money going? Is it coming back to our students and our group and our community, or is it improving the industry in any which way? You know what they're doing? It's genius. If I was there, I would do it too. What they're doing is they're taking their $100 million of profit and they're buying other software companies that we use so they can extract more revenue from us. They're not investing in us. They're figuring out how to price manipulate and make it so my students pay more and more and more for their services as they take more and more of our money. And so one of the things that we did is I said, I'm going to own things with my community. So what we just did, this took about two years, we launched our own title company in all 50 states and I gave ownership of it to my community. That's a company that in 2027 will probably net about $25 million net. And as we make that $25 million, we will use that money to buy companies that my students utilize and give ownership back to the student base. Now why is that? It's because we are changing the way that people operate in this industry or getting rid of all the backstabbing, getting rid of all the bad things that are going on in the industry. We're standardizing processes and procedures and we're creating new ways to make money as an industry. That was never a dream of mine. I just said I need to change the industry. It started at 3,000 people in a Prius and now I'm building companies and giving ownership to 20,000 people. And now what's happening is they're coming to us and going, hey, Pace, we're all buying insurance for all these properties. Why don't we just go launch our own insurance company? You imagine 20,000 real estate investors, how many insurance checks we write on a monthly basis. Why do we not own that together? And I look at that and I go that is building a community. That is building products that the community owns, controls, navigates and changes the way that the industry is handled. Up until about four or five years ago, we reacted to what Fidelity Title did. We reacted to all these big conglomerates and software companies did, but we realized that as we band together as a community, we are the ones that hold all the leverage. We can navigate where our profit goes and we can navigate what companies we build to go and change the entire industry. Last year, our community did $20 billion in transactions. And we gave all the profit on those transactions away to title companies and software companies. What is happening in your industry? Where's all your students' money going? What software are they using that you should own? And not only should you own it, you should own it with them. And the reason being is because now you have a loyal base of people with one common goal, rather than me, myself and I. And this is the next phase of where I'm going with my community. You're going to start seeing, if you were in real estate, you'd start seeing, my community members are now stepping on more stages than non-community members. So you look at the whole entire real estate industry, there's about 500 stages every single year. My community members are on over 300 of them already. In two years, we will have a member of our community on all 500 stages every single year. Why is that important? It's important because your students need to become leaders in your community, so that your new students that join have somebody to rely on. And I look at the model of every six months, right? Somebody joins, somebody will join your mentorship, and after six months, they've learned everything you taught them, and now where are they? You just got rid of one of the most important people in your community. Now, here's the thing that we're doing, and this is one thing that I'll break down for you, it's kind of cool. For the longest time in real estate, people would do these challenges. They go, watch me get a deal in 12 hours. I did this park bench challenge where I go, all right, get rid of my cell phone, no shoes. I'm literally like, I'm not even going to wear underwear, hell, I don't care. I'm going to sit on a park bench in a random city, and I'm going to, within 10 hours, get a real estate deal and make like $15,000 in 10 hours. But at some point, like, is that not kind of lame? Like, is it surprising that somebody that's been doing real estate for 11 years could go do that? So what we did is we crafted something that we are breaking a world record as a community. We have something we do every November, it's called No One Left Behind. Now, listen to this, this is so critical. If you don't know the success rate of your students in your community, how can you improve it? How can you improve it if you don't know the success rate? Do you know what the industry average in real estate is for success in mentorships and programs? Somebody joins, they pay money, how many of them get their money back? Anybody have a guess? It's 6%. By the way, where do I get this from? This is a scary word for people in coaching. I have a friend at the FTC, they keep track of all these things. So, 6%. 6% of people buy a thing and take action and get their money back. Do you know what our success rate is in our community? It's 82%. That's insane. But why is that? It's because every year what we do is we have something called the No One Left Behind Challenge. So people like Chino, people like Thomas, people in my community, all the people that have never gotten a deal done, all band up on a team, and we go and make sure that those people over the last year, we make sure they all get a deal done. We call that the No One Left Behind Challenge. We have the world record right now is 1,000 people in one week got their first deal. It's a world record. Guinness World Record. So think about what are you doing collectively as a community to lead your industry? Yes. But how do you get your success rate higher and higher and higher and higher? Do you know where most of my sales come from? Chino, have you ever introduced somebody to the community? How many people have you think you've brought into the community? Just you. A lot. Nothing travels faster than the speed of sound. And if your success rate is unknown to you and you're sitting there going, how do I get my funnel? I make my 2025 sales from my 2024 fulfillment. Remember that. And I watch too many people in the coaching industry, especially real estate. It's disgusting. It's horrible, in fact. People go create a $25,000 thing and the person who they paid doesn't even show up to one Zoom call. That is happening in this industry. This is why we as coaches get a bad rap. Do we all agree? And so this is why I don't like being a coach or I don't like being called a mentor because I get muddied around with those people. I want to be known as a community builder. And I want Chino and Thomas and all of my thousands of members. I want them to be more important to me in their local geography than I could ever be. Now, you don't need 20,000 people, guys. Did I start my mentorship with 20,000 people? No, I started with a little Prius and 3,000 people on my Instagram stories. I just was willing to do the hard things. Now, let's see how much time do I have? Okay, I got a little bit of time. Perfect. Okay, your students are the future of your industry, not you. At some point, the baton has to be passed along. And this is what I told Josiah. I said Josiah, when people come in here, I am not developing winners. I'm developing leaders. I want people to lead the industry. I want people to be recognized in their local area. This is a really fun one. Every year, I do something called community camp. Now, this is crazy. I don't think everybody's going to do this. But when you help somebody else get a real estate deal in my mentorship or in my community, you get a ticket to go to community camp. Community camp is at my house in Montana. We spend three days on a lake. We go to Glacier National Park. We do anything but talk about real estate. And what's cool is that the only way to get there is not by paying your way there. It's by helping somebody else in the community get over their lack of getting success. And so what happens is you create a culture of we don't leave people behind. We do not leave people behind because so many people will buy your videos thinking that that's going to change their life when the reality is it's a physical human being in your community that will. Now, here's a really fun thing that we do as well. Let me see if I can get to this part. Okay. You see these little badges right here? Now, this is something that I believe Russell Brunson has taken on. I've spent a lot of time with Russell over the last year and a half. And I watched him build Prime Mover. I'm so excited about Prime Mover. And I said, you cannot charge people a yearly fee because you need people to lead Prime Mover. You can't just create successful people and then say goodbye. You're not paying them anymore. You need to create a way for them to make money with each other the longer they stay in the community. They need to have incentivization. So what I do is every, I have semesters, so I treat my community like a college. Okay. You pay one time, you come in. And every quarter we come up with new merit badges. Anybody have a Boy Scout in here? Okay. Cool. Did you enjoy doing Boy Scouts or did you hate it like I did? If there were girls in Boy Scouts, I would have had a lot more fun. It was just a bunch of dudes. My parents wouldn't let me get my driver's license or go on dates until I had my Eagle Scout. And so I got my merit badges when I was younger. I had to get them. And when I got older, I said, man, you know what's interesting is that as my students scale, right, they get their first deal, their second deal, their third deal, they start asking questions that need like full dedicated courses. And so one of those questions is how do I hire my first person? How do I scale my business? How do I raise capital? How do I buy an RV park? How do I buy a multifamily? And I go, man, either I can provide this information to them or they can go somewhere else. If they go somewhere else, are they going to help me lead my community? And so what I did is I started building merit badges that I release every quarter. And those merit badges are taught by other specific people inside the community. So for example, Chino, I'll give you a good example. Chino's model, everybody's a little bit different in real estate, but Chino buys real estate with no cash, no credit, no credentials. That's what I teach people. And he puts people who are, what's your main target audience? You've got single mothers who are trying to escape what they're going through. Maybe they're escaping abuse and you house them. You also have people that are sleeping in their cars. They have a job. They might be like a Walmart greeter or they might work at Target and they can't afford where they're currently living. And so they're sleeping in their car and you help house them by giving them rooms for rent, correct? Okay, so that's a dedicated model called co-living. It's going to take the whole real estate world by storm, by the way. So if my students want to learn that specific topic, don't you think it would be smart for me to tell Chino, hey, Chino, I need you to build a 12-week class and we need to deliver it to the students next semester. So every semester there's new classes coming out, new classes coming out, new classes coming out. And so for the next, I have already the next eight years planned of merit badges. I have over 400 merit badges planned for my community. So think about this in your individual. Do you ever feel like in your course that you built, you felt like, man, I didn't give them everything in my course. I feel like there's so much more I want to give them. You ever feel like that? How many people feel like that when you build your course? The way I solve that is by building semesters. I solve that 100% by building semesters. And what's cool is my first course I ever built for Sub2, which is the name of my community, Sub2, was done on Kleenex boxes, on a laptop, and I didn't have a charger for it. So I had to take it over to this charger and bring it back over every day, and I would record everything. It wasn't with a professional microphone or a camera. It was on a laptop. Do you think I've rebuilt that course? No. Because where they get all their value is now as the semesters get released and the new classes that come out. Now what's cool about that is that now you've got long-term continuation. And more importantly, you're taking some of the leadership from your community and you're having them teach classes which then teaches them how to be better leaders and help you take the burden off your shoulders. Does that make sense? Okay. So I do the semesters. I break down the avatars. We create a lot of fun events. I just went to Alaska. Again, I do a lot of things as rewards, and a lot of the rewards are like, what are the problems we're having in the community? How many people call your Zoom every week that you do with your people? You call it a Q&A. Eh. I'm LDS, so what do we do on Monday nights? Family home evening. Do you know what I call my Q&A? Sub 2 family night. The words we use in our communities are very important. You call them a student, you create distance between you and them. Oh, this is my student. This is my student. This is my student. This is my student. It's a community member. It's a leader, right? Your Q&A should never be called a Q&A. Who wants...do you guys want to show up to a Q&A? Then why would you call it a Q&A? Isn't it funny how we use the wrong words? I call my sub...mine sub 2 family night. You guys can steal that, whatever you want to do with it, but I call mine sub 2 family night. Guess what everybody does? They all text during the week. Dude, I'll see you on sub 2 family night. I'll see you on sub 2... Never once has somebody ever texted their friend in your mentorship and said, I'll see you on the Q&A tomorrow. I'm so excited. It's sub 2 family night. And you treat it as a family atmosphere. Okay? Cool. A couple more things. We do...we gamify things. We do retreats. We do all sorts of fun things. But we also choose a charity that we as a community go after and raise money for. Last year, the name of the charity was Family Promise. So did you guys know that 1 in 30 children in America sleep in their parents' car because their parents can't afford housing? 1 in 30 children that you see. Your kids that go to school, everybody has kids that go to school here in America? 1 in 30 of the kids that they interact with sleeps in their parents' car. Okay? So what we do is we say, hey, look, this is a housing problem. We're going to go and supply housing to a charity. What do you think that does to loyalty in your community? What do you think that does with longevity and the commitment of where you're going as a community? Right? So many of you guys, maybe this room, maybe people out there on Zoom land. So many of those people. Oh, this is great. Man, what does sub 2 mean? Wondering the same thing. That's so funny. Sub 2 is a strategy of buying real estate. You guys can look it up on my YouTube channel. So many of you guys will call these coaching calls or Q&As. It creates a disconnect. Okay? It creates a massive disconnect. Other words that I prefer people not use, student is a big one. We don't use the word student. We don't use the word mentorship. I tell people on my sub 2 family night when I open up, I go, hey, guys, if you're brand new to the community, I just want to let you know. My name is Pace Morby. I'm sure you are aware of that. But a couple of things you probably are not aware of. You did not buy a mentorship. And you did not buy a course. If you think that the videos in this mentorship in this course are going to change your life, you are going to be sorely mistaken. The first thing I say every Tuesday night, and if you guys are relying on your videos in your course to change their life, you do not have a community. You do not have a community. What you need is local leadership that will take them under their wing. Chino, when do you guys do your meetups? By the way, I don't tell them when to do the meetups. He's a leader. He makes his own decisions. Chino, when do you guys do your meetups? Eight to nine times a month they meet locally. What do you guys do? Okay, so on Saturday mornings, you guys help the newbies get their first deal done. Is that correct? And then you have a meetup where you let people bring their spouse, their kids. You guys do water balloon fights, picnics, hikes, all that kind of stuff. Correct? Okay, so they take, guys, the words we choose are important. I did not tell him to call it that. He calls it the sub two family picnic. Do you see how leadership comes from the very top and the words you choose are very, very important. I would go home and I would say, do I really have a community? Or am I just using the catchphrase that everybody else is using? Think about that. And then sit down and write down all the words that you are choosing that you wouldn't want to be used against you. Do you want to be called a student? Or do you want to be called a member or a leader? I don't call Chino even a member. I call him a leader. Same thing with Thomas. How many Chino, how many Chinos do you think we have around the country, by the way? And are you friends with them? How many friends do you think you have in our community? Hundreds. Hundreds around the country. So if I need to get a deal done in Denver, let's say, with a seller named Eric, do you think I ever have to go to a forum that fakes itself as a community ever again? F. Steve, right? On the count of three, everybody say F. Steve. One, two, three, F. Steve. That's right. All right. Now in closing, here's what I want to tell you guys. I have so many stories and so many nitty gritty things. I'm obsessed with this topic. In fact, Russell Brunson, who is such a big hero of mine, he told me last time, he came to one of my retreats in Scottsdale. He said, man, you've shown up for me, I showed up for you. I had 10 other things going on and I made sure I showed up for you. But I changed a lot of the ways I was doing things based on how you're doing your community. And I said, would you let me do you a favor? Can I come into Prime Mover? Who's joining Prime Mover, by the way? How many people are going to be in Prime Mover? Okay. What's that? All of you. Oh, everybody. Yeah. Okay. Let me ask the people at home, how many people are going to be in Prime Mover? Okay. F. Steve. Guys, give me an F. Steve in the comments, please. Steve has no idea how important he was. And what's cool is I could have let Steve deflate me, but now I've got 10,000 people at home saying F. Steve. All right. So here's what I promise you guys. I'm a visual person. I'm a storyteller. One answer to a question usually takes me 42 minutes. That's my average in my sub two family night. Somebody asked me a question. It takes me 42 minutes to answer it. Why? Because that's what people need. They need to hear the why and where this came from and why that connected to here. And then boom, they never have to ask that question ever again. What I told Russell is I said, as he launches the Prime Mover community, please let me come in there and show and lift the whole thing. But I did $100 million in real estate transactions in my business with people in my community. And this is what changes the game because now guess what my students call me? Do they call me their mentor? Friend, homie, more importantly, partner. Every one of my community members that owns the title company with me, the software companies, do they go around and say, man, Pace is the greatest mentor? Or do they go around saying Pace is my business partner? So I would suggest doing the things that you sell and turning your students into partners. These people will be the greatest people on the planet Earth. They will pick up the baton. They will help you build that community. Otherwise, if you don't do it this way, I watch my other friends do this and they kick people out of their community, which it's not a community, they kick people out. And what happens is they go and create their own competition. It fragments and then you don't change the industry. Now you guys are fighting against each other. What we've done is we have the largest land development in the world. We have the largest RV park owner, the largest mobile home park guy, the largest burr guy, the largest Airbnb guy, the largest skyscraper guy, all of them are inside of our community. And what's been so fun for us, by the way, is we've created zooms. This is the last thing I'll say. I still have 32 seconds, so I'll finish early. Our community is now 51% women. 51% women. 51% women. How is that possible? Because I cultivate, and where did I learn this? I grew up in a family of 12 kids. I have eight sisters. I think I know 1% about women. Maybe one day when I go to Heaven, Heavenly Father will tell me all the other 99% I don't understand about women. But I cultivated women leaders, and women leaders are the greatest leaders you will ever see. The most unbelievable leaders on the planet. And 51% of people in our community are women leaders. Why? Because we give them dedicated space. We have zooms dedicated to women. So I would identify this with you as well. I also have zooms dedicated to our Spanish speakers. We have a Latino portion of our community. You go to that zoom. He's one of the leaders in that zoom too. Do you see how they take it upon themselves? I don't have to be the one that's doing all the thing. It's a common goal, a common march all the way across the country. All 50 states and 29 countries we are in. And this is what happens when you build a community. They take it upon their shoulders. I don't tell him what to do. He does it on his own because he's incentivized to lead. And he was born to lead. Our last keynote speakers in the other room. I'm so excited to introduce you guys here in a few minutes, but I've got probably 15 more minutes with you guys to share one last cool thing. This is something we started doing at Funnel Hacking Live four or five years ago. It was something I saw Tony Robbins do. How many of you guys have ever been to Tony Robbins' date with Destiny? Yeah! Yeah, so I went to date with Destiny probably, how old is Aiden? Aiden's club was part of Aiden's, so 14 years ago went to date with Destiny. And remember, at the end of the event, he had everybody write a letter to themselves. And then he still did an envelope and then shipped it back to us like six months later to read about the commitments we made while we were together. And it was like one of the coolest things, number one, to sit down and actually think through that and write out a letter to myself. But number two, when I got it in the mail like six, eight, twelve months later where it was, it was one of the most amazing things ever. So four or five years ago at Funnel Hacking Live, I thought let's do the exact same thing. So we did a really cool exercise. In fact, if we want to throw up the slide, I'll show you what it looks like. We had everyone write a hand out letter to everybody. And the very first time nobody knew what it was, I asked them to start filling out the set, fill out step number one, step number two, and we folded it up and then I gave them all an envelope and they wrote their home address on it. It was a self-addressed stamped envelope. We had them put that letter in the envelope and then pass it to the middle. We took all these envelopes, like 5,000 envelopes with people's letters themselves, and then we held them in a huge box for like nine months. And then before the next Funnel Hacking Live came, we shipped them all out to everybody. And it was one of the coolest experiences in the world, people started getting these and they started reading them and realizing that first, for some people, it's like, I set this goal, this intention, and I got it. Or other people were like, I set this goal and I didn't quite do it, and re-inspired them to get back into moving forward and pursuing these things. And a whole bunch of other just amazing, magical things came from it. And that was my biggest fear as we started at Funnel Hacking Live International. I'm like, I want to have this experience because it's one of the coolest things in the world. I was like, but how do I get everyone to write a letter and then seal it and send it back to themselves? And it was just kind of, I don't know how to do it. So I had what I think is the second best idea, which is to have you guys do a digital version. So this is what the letter looks like at Funnel Hacking Live, if you see it. And I asked a couple of questions on the letter on the very first question he says, on insert date. We're going to be gathering together again as Funnel Hackers. And the question is, what will that happen in your life, both personally and professionally, for you to feel happy about your progress? And the way you write this letter is not just like, Dear Russell, I'm going to feel awesome. I want you to write it as if you've already achieved it. So, hey, Dear Russell, six months ago at Funnel Hacking Live International, you set a goal that you were going to figure out exactly how to create your one to many presentation. You were going to create a level 10 offer and you committed yourself. You were going to do a live webinar every single week for next 12 weeks. And by week number six, you finally cracked the code. You got it converting. And then last four or five months since then you've made X amount of dollars. That's amazing. Okay. So we talk about what we would have to do to be happy with the progress. And the second one, two, is what are you willing to do or to not do to make that vision become a reality? Okay. Some of you guys, you may have to be getting out of your comfort zone. Some of you guys may have to cut out things of your life. Some of you guys may need to find new friend groups, new peer groups. A lot of things might have to happen. So the first is like, what is the thing of what you're trying to accomplish with the goal that you've already achieved the way you're writing? And then number two is coming back to figure out what are the things that you must do or not do to make this vision become a reality? So that's the game plan.
